subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: Within the terms of the hypothesis, it is difficult to appreciate why a set of ideas and practices which results from replication and infection should have any more significance evolutionarily than one which is purposely chosen and consciously developed.
rationale: Challenges the selection-primary framing by arguing memetic selection has no special evolutionary significance over conscious human choice.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: Memetics, operating by a process assumed to be analogous to natural selection (requiring the involuntary assimilation of genotypes), brackets-out the elements of elective adaptation and performative alteration from the process
rationale: Argues memetic selection illegitimately brackets out human agency, performance, and elective adaptation — undermining the selection mechanism as theorized.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: The fittest memes with the greatest longevity will be those with a high degree of identity between the model and its replica. The resulting tendency will be for any impulse towards change in cultural terms to be folded back on itself underneath the weight of unrealisable stereotypes.
rationale: Critiques memetic fitness as producing only unrealizable stereotypes that suppress real cultural variation, exposing a structural flaw in fitness as criterion.

subject: concept:memetic-fitness
quote: the meme paradigm as a prototype-stereotype. Importantly, in spite of the likelihood of idiosyncratic variations in any individual's reception of a meme, in Dawkins' elaboration of it the meme can only ever be a stereotype
rationale: Develops a critical operational read of memetic fitness as forcing prototype-stereotype identity at the cost of real variation.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: For Dawkins the medium of transmission of a meme is not significant – the same meme may be transmitted by various means (albeit while attracting inconsequential variations along the way)
rationale: Identifies Dawkins' refusal to treat medium as significant — which is precisely what the medium-shapes-criteria claim asserts. Critique of memetics surfaces this gap.

subject: question:medium-and-manipulation
quote: The essential formal connection of a meme with its respective medium is never taken into material consideration in this theory, which hence becomes an ideology of pure content, presupposing unprecedented liquidity in its transmission.
rationale: Frames memetics' core weakness as severing meme from medium — directly engages the medium-shapes-gates question.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: In any credible science of information however, such a theory should be rejected on the basis of Maxwell's second law of thermodynamics, recognising that no information-bearing system can operate without a memory-storage overhead, and the consequent investment of energy.
rationale: Argues from thermodynamics that information must always have a material medium — supports the claim that every gate has an attached medium.

subject: tradition:memetics
quote: My discussion offers a kindred critique to the contemporary 'science' of memetics, whose currency we owe to the eminent sociobiologist Richard Dawkins.
rationale: Paper is a sustained engagement with memetics as a research tradition — its central object of critique.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: there is no empirical test which will enable us to distinguish between relative copy infidelity and meme diversity
rationale: Identifies a fundamental empirical undecidability that undermines selection's operationalization in memetics.

subject: mechanism:selection
quote: there is no role left for conscious criticism in the reception and transmission of memes – there are only levels of susceptibility, and degrees of (secondary) variation
rationale: Critiques the selection mechanism as conceived in memetics for eliminating any role for conscious receiver criticism.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: why biologists have not in the forty-seven years since the publication of Dawkins' theory been able offer any empirical evidence whatsoever to support their concepts of what a meme is
rationale: Strong empirical challenge: no evidence has accumulated for the basic unit on which selection-side memetic theory rests.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: Doesn't it feel more like a piece of science fiction, which obfuscates a deeper motivation towards a revision of the role of biology, and of sociobiologists in particular, in terms of "political understanding"
rationale: Casts the selection-primary framing as ideologically motivated rather than empirically grounded.

subject: concept:manipulation-surface
quote: If memes are indices of potential balance and harmony (through uncritical assimilation), this entails a fantastic promise of social control through the control of meanings.
rationale: Identifies the social-control / manipulation surface implicit in memetics' uncritical-assimilation framing.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: The idea of 'cultural evolution' proposed by memetics is and can only ever be fundamentally ahistorical, because it privileges continuity over discontinuity – the transcendental properties of memes over the transformative potential of praxis.
rationale: Argues memetic selection is structurally ahistorical and ignores discontinuous human praxis — a deep challenge to the selection-as-evolution analogy.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: in the idea 'Catholicism', there are certain key associations which inhere in all instances; e.g., the centrality of the Holy Trinity, the proscription on contraception, the divine authority of the Pope, etc. In Dawkins' version, memes are potentiated
rationale: Critiques the fitness/transcendent-essence move in memetics as illegitimately reifying contingent cultural associations.

subject: question:truth-value-placement
quote: Dawkins is confident in his general ability to make moral distinctions between 'good' memes, e.g., 'cooperation', 'charity', the 'benign' ideas shared by scientists, etc.; and 'bad' or 'doctrinaire' memes
rationale: Engages directly with where truth/moral value enters the memetic model — and critiques Dawkins' self-serving placement.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: the dominant function in meme transmission is that of heredity – it is the stability of the meme's core properties that ensure its longevity. Where variations outstrip heredity, the meme fails the test of survival – it loses its identity
rationale: Exposes a tension within memetic selection: heredity dominates such that genuine variation makes the meme cease to exist — selection cannot accommodate praxis.

subject: mechanism:manufactured-injection
quote: there are only levels of susceptibility, and degrees of (secondary) variation. There is also implied the possibility of immunisation against deleterious ideas
rationale: Memetics frames belief acquisition as passive infection — closely related to manufactured-injection dynamics, though critiqued here.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: the idea that the algorithm of the method of use of a particular tool for a particular purpose takes the initial form of a discrete information entity (i.e., one that may be copied in the form, let's say, of a mental representation) is hardly an adequate
rationale: Challenges the discrete-unit / copy-and-spread framing that the complexity-virality inverse claim implicitly assumes.

subject: concept:memetic-fitness
quote: what makes memes so infectious […] is a certain transcendent quality. For instance, in the idea 'Catholicism', there are certain key associations which inhere in all instances
rationale: Engages substantively with how memetic fitness is theorized as transcendent essence, then critiques that move.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: Mechanistic principles such as the 'transmission of ideas', or 'the idea that we implant in the learner's head', are just not appropriate to the field of linguistic communications
rationale: Linguistics-based challenge: ideas are contextualized through signification, not mechanistically selected/transmitted as discrete units.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: the activities and artefacts of cultural process are not reducible to the logical elements of a closed system, such as might be described by analogues of neural networks, or modelled by cybernetics
rationale: Argues cultural processes are not reducible to closed selection systems — a deep challenge to the selection-primary frame.

subject: concept:handle-ability
quote: When ideas are exchanged they are transformed – radically in terms of form, and socially in terms of content; that is, they are contextualised
rationale: Suggests that what makes an idea travel is its contextualization / fit-to-mind, akin to handle-ability rather than abstract complexity.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: memetics presupposes that ideas are like free-floating entities, without formal/textual specificities (i.e., without a history, and without a code). Individual memes are presumed to be autonomous from any particular instance of them
rationale: Core ontological critique: memetic fitness assumes free-floating decontextualized entities — but no such things exist, so the criterion is incoherent.

subject: source:memetics-critique
quote: In its dependence on performance and active imitation, the assumed coherence and stability of the meme's core semantic properties seems to be highly problematic
rationale: Selection requires stable units to act on; the paper shows units are never stable under performance — undermining the selection mechanism.

_note: Paper repeatedly engages a 'medium attached to every gate' critique that is the inverse of Dawkins' meme-medium-independence claim. Heavy engagement with Blackmore and Dennett. Potential catalog gap: a concept of 'discrete-unit assumption' or 'replicator ontology' which the paper specifically attacks.
